meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Learn how to overcome debugging hurdles by building a portfolio. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for aspiring AI builders.
content.answer_block: Stop treating bugs as failures; treat them as documentation for your growth. By building a portfolio, you shift from struggling with syntax to mastering architecture. Use AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the noise, allowing you to focus on the high-level logic that defines a professional developer.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the terminal glows with a wall of red text, and the silence of your room feels heavy with frustration. You’ve spent hours chasing a missing semicolon or a logic error that refuses to surrender. This isn't just a bug; it is a wall between you and the developer you are destined to become.

But here is the secret: even the best engineers don't 'fix' bugs—they architect their way out of them. By focusing on purposeful portfolio building, you stop debugging in isolation and start building systems that are inherently resilient. It’s time to move beyond the syntax struggle and start shipping real-world projects.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Scope
steps.description: Narrow your project to a single, high-impact feature to ensure you don't get lost in the weeds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Help me define a scope for a beginner-level web app that solves a specific student problem (like a study timer) while keeping the architecture modular and easy to debug.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose a 3-file architecture for a simple task manager app.'

steps.title: Setup the Project Skeleton
steps.description: Create a clean directory structure that separates concerns, making future debugging significantly easier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a folder structure for a React app that follows best practices for scalability. Explain why each folder exists.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'mkdir -p src/components src/hooks src/services && touch src/App.tsx'

steps.title: Automate Logic with Claude
steps.description: Use AI to write the boilerplate, then manually review it to understand the flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, commented function to handle API data fetching with error states included.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a fetch wrapper with try/catch blocks and loading state management.'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Debugging
steps.description: Integrate logging and assertions early so that when bugs happen, the code tells you exactly where.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Show me how to implement robust error logging in my current project that won't crash the UI.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add console.error logging and error boundaries to the main layout component.'

steps.title: Refactor for Portfolio Readiness
steps.description: Clean up your code and write a README that highlights the challenges you solved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my code and suggest three professional improvements that would make a senior dev impressed with this portfolio piece.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review 'Identify potential refactors for cleaner code in the current directory.'

pro_tips: Always commit your code before asking an AI to debug; it allows you to roll back if the AI introduces a hallucinated dependency.
If you can't explain why a piece of code works, delete it and rewrite it until you can—this is the fastest way to learn.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude: Explain your bug to the AI in plain English before asking for the solution.
Prioritize building one project you can demo live over three projects that only exist as local terminal scripts.
Document your 'aha!' moments in a simple README.md; employers hire people who can articulate their problem-solving process.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify, share the link on LinkedIn, and start planning your second project with a new, more complex technical requirement.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the mindset to stop struggling and start building. Every line of code you write is a brick in the foundation of your future career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
